title: Consonants: Dental Sounds (तवर्ग)
description: Now we learn the dental consonants — sounds produced with the tongue touching the back of the upper teeth. English speakers often confuse these with the retroflexes, so pay close attention! The five letters are त, थ, द, ध, and न.
keyPoints: त (ta), थ (tha), द (da), ध (dha), न (na) — the dental group
Touch your tongue to the BACK of your upper front teeth for these sounds
These are softer than retroflex sounds (ट, ठ, ड, ढ, ण)
थ and ध are the aspirated versions of त and द

hint: Draw a small loop on the left, then a vertical line going down on the right. Finish with the headline (shirorekha) connecting on top.

title: Dental vs. Retroflex
explanation: Hindi distinguishes between dental sounds (त, थ, द, ध, न) and retroflex sounds (ट, ठ, ड, ढ, ण). Dentals are softer — your tongue touches the teeth. Retroflexes are harder — your tongue curls back. Mixing them up changes the meaning of words!

contextSentenceTranslation: Put the food on the plate.
memoryTrick: 'Thali' — you already know this word from Indian restaurants!

text: Which letter is the aspirated dental 'd' sound (dha)?

hint: Draw a curved shape that looks like a reverse 'C' with a vertical stroke. Add the headline on top.

title: Dental Sounds — Recap
takeaways: You learned 5 dental consonants: त (ta), थ (tha), द (da), ध (dha), न (na)
Dental sounds are made with the tongue touching the back of the upper teeth
Don't confuse dentals (त-group) with retroflexes (ट-group) — they sound and look different!
New words: तारा (star), थाली (plate), दरवाज़ा (door), धन (wealth), नदी (river)
